_note: lint-changed-files.sh is PostToolUse (matcher: Write|Edit), best-effort/informational — never blocks. This dev/CI-authoring sandbox has no linter toolchain installed (ruff, eslint, shellcheck, go, cargo, pyright all confirmed absent via `command -v`; no eslint config present at repo root either — confirmed by direct search), so the delegate path (a real lint run producing additionalContext) cannot be exercised here without fabricating output; per the iteration's instruction, only the no-op paths are covered. Each case's throwaway case_dir (used as CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR) is guaranteed fresh, so it never contains pyproject.toml/.ruff.toml/an eslintrc/go.mod/Cargo.toml regardless of host tooling — the .py/.ts/.go/.rs no-op cases below are therefore deterministic in any environment, not just this one. The .sh extension is deliberately NOT covered here: unlike the other extensions, its shellcheck branch has no config-file guard (command-presence only, `command -v shellcheck`), and CI (.github/workflows/publish.yml) apt-get-installs shellcheck while this authoring sandbox does not — a 'no shellcheck present' no-op case authored here would be expected to flip to a real (unverified) lint delegate run under CI, which is exactly the kind of environment-fragile assertion the iteration's brief says not to fabricate. Revisit .sh coverage when this hook is ported (Phase 2 iteration 2.5), either with a staged sample verified shellcheck-clean against a real shellcheck binary, or an environment-conditional case. Every case below was verified by direct invocation of the unmodified hooks/post-tool/lint-changed-files.sh.
